response.justification: Candidate is an experienced generalist with strong web/full-stack credentials (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) but the resume shows only a brief, ~6-month C++ stint at National Instruments around 2000-2001 and no recent C++ work. The role requires hands-on C++/STL architecture, multi-threaded networked systems, IEC 62443 / SDL secure engineering, MQTT, and industrial protocol exposure — none of which appear on the resume. Additionally, the timeline contains substantial internal inconsistencies (overlapping long-term roles, employment dates predating stated education, technologies like Node.js/React/AWS CDK attributed to roles in the early 2000s) which undermine credibility for a senior, security-sensitive position.
strengths: Long, broad engineering career spanning web, embedded, and distributed systems
Some low-latency / async / micro-services experience (DLang at Sociomantic) relevant to performance work
Comfortable with agile/TDD/CI-CD practices and mentoring (workshops, dojos, courses)
concerns: Minimal and dated C++/STL experience; primary stack is JS/TS and DLang, not C++
No evidence of IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or industrial automation protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
Resume timeline and tech-stack attributions are internally inconsistent, raising credibility questions

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity / IEC 62443 / MQTT exposure
Breadth of senior engineering experience and mentoring
